title: Compact Language Models via Pruning and Knowledge Distillation (Minitron)
claim: Depth+width+head+MLP pruning with KD-based retraining on <3% of original data; 8B/4B derived from 15B need up to 40x fewer training tokens per model than from-scratch; 1.8x compute saving for the family; up to 16% MMLU improvement over from-scratch training under ISO-COMPUTE accounting, NOT equal tokens (re-verified 2026-07-11 at Sec 4.3/Table 11: 4B pruned+distilled on 100B KD tokens scores 37.81 MMLU vs 26.24 for the 150B-token from-scratch twin; scratch baseline gets more tokens because distillation pays teacher forward passes; asterisks mark iso-compute rows); Minitron comparable to Mistral-7B/Gemma-7B/Llama-3-8B

title: LLM Pruning and Distillation in Practice: The Minitron Approach
claim: Practice recipe: teacher correction (lightly fine-tune teacher on the distillation dataset when original pretraining data unavailable) before KD; evaluates depth pruning vs joint hidden/attention/MLP width pruning; Llama-3.1-8B->4B and Mistral-NeMo-12B->Minitron-8B (state-of-the-art at release), weights open

title: Sheared LLaMA: Accelerating Language Model Pre-training via Structured Pruning
claim: Targeted structured pruning (layers/heads/intermediate/hidden to a target shape, end-to-end) + dynamic batch loading (domain-loss-driven data re-mixing); LLaMA2-7B pruned to 1.3B/2.7B beats Pythia/INCITE/OpenLLaMA/concurrent TinyLlama at equivalent sizes using ~3% of from-scratch compute — accounting caveat: the 3% excludes the donor's pretraining compute

title: LLM-Pruner: On the Structural Pruning of Large Language Models
claim: Gradient-based structural pruning over dependency-coupled groups, task-agnostic; recovery via LoRA in ~3h on 50k samples; at 20% pruning ratio LLaMA/Vicuna/ChatGLM retain 'satisfactory' zero-shot classification/generation

title: SliceGPT: Compress Large Language Models by Deleting Rows and Columns
claim: Computational-invariance-based structured compression: orthogonal transform of the residual stream absorbed into adjacent weight matrices, then PCA-ranked rows/columns deleted (embedding dimension reduced); uses a DIFFERENT orthogonal Q_ell per block ('signals at different blocks... were not aligned'), inserting explicit Q^T_{ell-1}Q_ell bridge matrices into each skip connection — small online DxD ops that 'cannot be pre-computed' — NOT one global residual-stream Q (re-verified 2026-07-11 at Sec 3.3/Fig 4); PCA objective is L2 reconstruction of calibration activations, not model-output error (Sec 3.4); 25% slicing keeps 99% zero-shot performance on LLAMA2-70B and OPT-66B but only 90% on Phi-2 (2.7B) — smaller models degrade more; inference cost down to 64-66% of dense

title: The Unreasonable Ineffectiveness of the Deeper Layers
claim: Layer pruning selected by inter-layer representational (angular) similarity + QLoRA healing: minimal QA-benchmark degradation until up to ~half the layers of some open-weight models are removed; interpreted as under-utilisation of deep layers or shallow-layer-dominant knowledge storage

title: MobileLLM: Optimizing Sub-billion Parameter Language Models for On-Device Use Cases
claim: At 125M/350M architecture dominates: deep-and-thin, embedding sharing, grouped-query attention, block-wise weight sharing; +2.7pp/+4.3pp over prior 125M/350M SOTA; API-calling correctness close to LLaMA-v2-7B

title: Distillation Scaling Laws
claim: Distillation scaling law predicting student performance from compute split across teacher/student; distillation beats supervised pretraining only up to a compute level that scales predictably with student size and only when a teacher exists or many students amortise it; capacity gap (stronger teacher -> worse student) explained as learning-capacity gap; compute-optimal recipes given

title: QuaRot: Outlier-Free 4-Bit Inference in Rotated LLMs
venue: arXiv 2024 (NeurIPS 2024 per community records - venue not confirmed at source)
claim: Per-component invariance accounting (HTML v2 fetched): RMSNorm(X)=RMSNorm(XQ^T)Q lets residual-stream rotations be absorbed into W_q/W_k/W_v/W_up/W_gate (as Q^T diag(alpha) W after pre-fusing RMSNorm gains) and W_down/W_out (as H W Q); online Hadamard REQUIRED before FFN down-projection (gating nonlinearity regenerates outliers); RoPE workaround (re-verified 2026-07-11): online head-wise Hadamards on QUERIES and KEYS after RoPE — matching rotations, attention scores invariant ('the existence of Pos prevents us from directly fusing the Hadamard matrix into W_q and W_k') — while VALUES take a separate path, a head-wise rotation pre-fused into W_v for KV-cache quantisation with no online transform; 4-bit LLaMA2-70B: <=0.47 WikiText-2 ppl loss, 99% zero-shot retained

title: SpinQuant: LLM Quantization with Learned Rotations
claim: Identifies rotation parameterisations that leave full-precision output invariant; learns rotations (Stiefel-manifold optimisation) instead of random ones — random rotation choice alone swings downstream zero-shot reasoning by up to 13 points at 4-bit; strong W4A4KV4 results incl. LLaMA-3-8B. R1-R4 taxonomy (which merge into weights vs run online) NOT verified at source this session (HTML fetch 404) — carried from abstract only

title: Signed-Permutation Coordinate Transport for RMSNorm Transformers
claim: With generic per-channel RMSNorm gain the exact gauge (symmetry) group of the coordinate system is the signed-permutation group B_d = S_d x {+-1}^d, NOT O(d); LayerNorm charts have permutation gauge S_d UP TO A GLOBAL SIGN FLIP (re-verified 2026-07-11); permutation-only alignment is symmetry-incomplete for RMSNorm models (worked example: SAE reconstruction NMSE 0.004 -> 1.08 under permutation-only vs signed-permutation transport); steering vectors, SAEs, top-k neuron sets, attribution lists must be co-transported. WEIGHT: very recent single-author preprint scoped to coordinate transport of interpretability artifacts across checkpoints — strict-gauge statement to be independently re-derived in P3-D-DD, not the final word on transformer-surgery invariance

title: Scaling Linear Mode Connectivity and Merging to Billion Parameter Pretrained Transformers
claim: Functionality-preserving weight transformations, jointly learned per model pair, align independently trained transformers onto low-loss-barrier linear interpolation paths: NEAR-ZERO barriers for medium-sized WikiText LMs (first near-barrier-free linear connectivity at that scale); only SMALL nonzero barriers for modern billion-parameter LLMs (re-verified 2026-07-11 — 'near-zero' does NOT apply at billion-parameter scale); ViT-L keeps >=69% ImageNet top-1 along the path. The stronger 'rotation hypothesis' framing (same function up to a residual-stream rotation learnable from one batch) seen only in search snippets - UNVERIFIED at source

title: The Linear Representation Hypothesis and the Geometry of Large Language Models
claim: Formalises linear representation of concepts; Euclidean inner product is the wrong geometry for concept directions — a 'causal inner product' (whitened basis) makes causally separable concepts orthogonal; validated on LLaMA-2; implies concept directions are generically NON-orthogonal in the naive residual-stream basis

title: Toy Models of Superposition
claim: Networks store more sparse features than dimensions as NON-orthogonal directions (superposition); phase transition governs when features enter superposition; polysemanticity is a strategic compression, not a failure — foundational reason no clean per-dimension concept ownership exists

title: Sparse Autoencoders Do Not Find Canonical Units of Analysis
claim: SAE stitching shows smaller SAEs are incomplete (larger SAEs contain novel latents, not just refinements); meta-SAEs decompose latents into interpretable parts ('Einstein' -> 'scientist'+'Germany'+'famous person'); conclusion: no canonical atomic feature set at any SAE size

title: Sparse Autoencoders Trained on the Same Data Learn Different Features
claim: 131K-latent SAE on Llama-3-8B: only ~30% of features shared across random seeds; TopK SAEs more seed-dependent than ReLU+L1; consistent across layers/datasets/architectures — SAE decompositions are pragmatic, not unique

title: A is for Absorption: Studying Feature Splitting and Absorption in Sparse Autoencoders
claim: Feature absorption: parent features stop firing where children absorb them (hierarchy fragmentation); validated across hundreds of LLM SAEs; not fixable by size/sparsity tuning; SAE latents unreliable as COMPLETE concept indicators — fatal for 'this latent = this concept everywhere' assumptions

title: Are Sparse Autoencoders Useful? A Case Study in Sparse Probing
claim: Across data-scarce, class-imbalanced, label-noise, and distribution-shift probing regimes, SAE probes occasionally beat baselines on individual datasets but no SAE(+ensemble) method consistently beats standard baselines

title: Sparse Feature Circuits: Discovering and Editing Interpretable Causal Graphs in Language Models
claim: Attribution-based discovery of causal circuits of SAE features (Gemma/Pythia); SHIFT: ablating human-judged task-irrelevant features improves classifier generalisation — the published template for interp-guided causal ablation-with-purpose (closest methodological ancestor of P3-E-DD-0 steps 1-2)

title: Negative Results for SAEs On Downstream Tasks and Deprioritising SAE Research (GDM mech-interp team)
claim: 2025-03-26: dense linear probes vastly outperform SAE probes incl. OOD harmful-intent detection; chat-data SAE finetuning closes only ~half the gap; GDM deprioritises fundamental SAE research (not 'SAEs are useless'); SAEs still useful for dataset debugging/spurious-correlation discovery

title: How Pruning Reshapes Features: Sparse Autoencoder Analysis of Weight-Pruned Language Models
claim: 3 model families x 2 pruning strategies x 6 sparsity levels: RARE (low-firing) SAE features survive pruning better than frequent ones (Spearman rho=-1.0 in 11/17 conditions); Wanda preserves feature geometry up to 3.7x better than magnitude pruning; pretrained SAEs transfer to Wanda-pruned models to 50% sparsity; geometric preservation does NOT predict causal importance

title: Pythia: A Suite for Analyzing Large Language Models Across Training and Scaling
claim: 16 models 70M-12B, identical public data in identical order, 154 checkpoints each + exact dataloader reconstruction — the controlled substrate for size-vs-capability curves and the second H-DD donor (Pythia-160M; LayerNorm, parallel blocks)

title: SmolLM2: When Smol Goes Big — Data-Centric Training of a Small Language Model
venue: arXiv (HuggingFace); COLM 2025 status not confirmed at source
claim: 1.7B trained ~11T tokens, multi-stage data-centric mixing (FineMath/Stack-Edu/SmolTalk introduced); outperforms Qwen2.5-1.5B and Llama-3.2-1B; 135M/360M siblings exist but the abstract does NOT state their token counts — do not attribute ~11T to the 135M donor without the paper's training details; the R-1/R-2 donor anchors and the size-vs-capability practice frontier at 100M-2B

title: Physics of Language Models Part 3.3: Knowledge Capacity Scaling Laws
venue: arXiv (Meta/MBZUAI); ICML 2025 status not confirmed at source
claim: LMs store max ~2 bits of factual knowledge per parameter (bit-complexity of stored tuples, controlled synthetic corpora), stable to int8 quantisation; GPT-2-with-RoPE matches/exceeds LLaMA/Mistral architectures at knowledge storage; MoE sparsity, insufficient exposure, and junk data reduce capacity; domain-name prepending increases it. USE LIMIT: a synthetic-conditions capacity estimate — NOT an upper bound on external-store bytes needed to backfill a parameter cut (a store additionally pays addressing/keys/schema/retrieval-index overhead)

title: Pre-training Large Memory Language Models with Internal and External Knowledge (LMLM)
claim: Title re-verified 2026-07-11 at HTML v2, which states verbatim 'LmLm stands for Large Memory Language Model' — NOT 'Limited Memory' as this entry previously said (the arXiv abs-page abstract snapshot fetched the same day still read 'Limited Memory Language Models', so the name appears to have changed between versions; use the v2 title). Pretraining corpus annotated with database lookups; retrieved factual spans MASKED FROM THE LOSS so the model learns targeted lookup instead of memorisation; small LMLMs competitive with substantially larger vanilla LLMs on standard benchmarks; facts editable/verifiable in the external DB. Closest H-DD prior art on the destination architecture — but train-time externalisation of a NEW model, NOT post-hoc identified-parameter removal from an existing donor

title: Memory Layers at Scale
claim: Trainable sparse key-value memory layers replacing FFN capacity: beat dense models given >2x the compute and MoE at matched resources, largest gains on factual tasks; scaled to 128B memory parameters over 1T training tokens — factual capacity is separable from dense compute and relocatable into a lookup structure

title: Does Localization Inform Editing? Surprising Differences in Causality-Based Localization vs. Knowledge Editing
claim: Causal-tracing localisation provides NO insight into which MLP layer is best to edit; editing success is essentially unrelated to where tracing places the fact — the standing threat to any 'semantically-identified parameters' program and the reason P3-E-DD-0 requires ablate/patch + restoration evidence, not localisation alone

title: Improving Language Models by Retrieving from Trillions of Tokens (RETRO)
venue: arXiv 2021/2022 (DeepMind; ICML 2022 status not confirmed at source)
claim: 7.5B RETRO with 2T-token retrieval DB (frozen BERT retriever + chunked cross-attention) matches GPT-3/Jurassic-1 on the Pile with 25x fewer parameters; RETROfit: a pretrained transformer can be rapidly retrofitted with retrieval — retrieval substitutes for parametric capacity at equal quality

title: To Memorize or to Retrieve: Scaling Laws for RAG-Considerate Pretraining
claim: 30M-3B models, varied pretraining tokens x retrieval-store size under fixed data budgets: retrieval improves all scales; 3-D scaling framework (params x tokens x retrieval corpus); optimal parametric-vs-retrieval allocation is task- and saturation-dependent, not universal

title: Model Unlearning via Sparse Autoencoder Subspace Guided Projections (SSPU)
claim: SAE-guided layer/feature selection + QR subspace construction + constrained optimisation steering activations into an 'irrelevant' subspace; -3.22% harmful-knowledge accuracy vs strongest baseline on WMDP-Cyber with utility retained; REMOVAL ONLY — no external-store backfill, no parameter deletion

title: The Super Weight in Large Language Models
claim: Pruning as few as ONE scalar parameter can destroy generation (perplexity up 3 orders of magnitude); identified data-free in a single forward pass; induce rare massive 'super activations' — surgery-fragility floor: any H-DD arm must protect identified super weights or explain their handling

title: Atlas: Few-shot Learning with Retrieval Augmented Language Models
venue: JMLR 2023 (KB record kb/records/arxiv_2208.03299.json - NOT re-verified at source this session)
claim: 11B retrieval-augmented model beats 540B PaLM on NaturalQuestions-64-shot (KB-recorded claim); joint retriever-reader training — second retrieval-substitutes-for-parameters datapoint

title: Llama SLayer 8B: Shallow Layers Hold the Key to Knowledge Injection
venue: EMNLP 2024 findings (KB record kb/records/arxiv_2410.02330.json - NOT re-verified at source this session)
claim: Layer-importance ranking for knowledge injection: shallow layers critical, deeper layers prunable for injection purposes (KB-recorded claim) — consistent with deeper-layers result

title: Evaluating and Designing Sparse Autoencoders by Approximating Quasi-Orthogonality
venue: arXiv (KB record kb/records/arxiv_2503.24277.json - NOT re-verified at source this session)
claim: SAE dictionaries are at best QUASI-orthogonal; norm-matching/adaptive-sparsity designs built on approximate orthogonality, confirming exact-orthogonal-block assumptions are unfounded (KB-recorded claim)

title: LMLM result magnitudes (which sizes match which larger models)
claim: Exact size-vs-match numbers for LMLM (e.g. which parameter count matches which vanilla size on TriviaQA/hallucination metrics) not extracted from abstract — UNVERIFIED pending full-text read in P3-D-DD
